state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Texas agriculture ranges from High Plains cotton to Rio Grande Valley citrus and winter vegetables—scale and diversity are unmatched in the lower 48.
fun_facts: Texas leads the nation in cattle inventory year after year.
Winter spinach, onions, and leafy crops from South Texas feed national chains.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Oklahoma anchors Great Plains beef and winter wheat, with growing specialty melon and peach farms east of I-35.
fun_facts: Pecan groves follow river corridors and remain culturally tied to holiday baking.
Watermelon festivals signal peak July–August harvests.
